# External Documentation and Guidelines

- this directory contains documentation related to snippets, guidelines, external dependencies and APIs, e.g. django, htmx, n8n etc.
- every dependency or system has its own subdirectory, which contains markdown files with information about the dependency or system, e.g. usage guidelines, best practices, etc.
- the documentation in this directory is meant to be used as a reference for developers when working with the respective dependencies or systems, and should be kept up to date with the latest information and best practices
- each sub directory for a framework or dependency should contain a README.md file that provides an overview of the contents and how to use the documentation in that subdirectory, as well as links to the relevant markdown files for specific topics or questions related to that framework or dependency
- split the documents into functional areas as needed, e.g. if there are multiple documents related to django, they can be organized into a `django/` subdirectory with separate markdown files for different aspects of django usage, such as `internationalization.md`, `admin.md`, `cotton.md`, etc. This way, developers can easily find the relevant information without having to sift through a single large document that covers all aspects of django and other dependencies together.
